concepts.title: The Five-Paragraph Structure
concepts.description: An article typically follows a five-paragraph structure: Introduction, Body Paragraph 1, Body Paragraph 2, Conclusion, and Closing. This structure helps to present a clear and organized argument or opinion.

concepts.title: The Importance of a Hook in the Introduction
concepts.description: A hook is a device used to grab the reader's attention in the introduction. It can be a quote, a statistic, or a thought-provoking question. A good hook sets the tone for the rest of the article and encourages the reader to continue reading.

concepts.title: The Use of Transitions in Article Writing
concepts.description: Transitions are words or phrases that connect ideas between sentences, paragraphs, or sections. They help to create a smooth flow of ideas and make the article more cohesive. Common transitions include however, therefore, meanwhile, and in addition.

concepts.title: The P.E.E.L. Method for Writing a Conclusion
concepts.description: The P.E.E.L. method is a mnemonic device that helps to remember the key elements of a conclusion: Point (reiterate the main point), Elaborate (provide evidence or examples), Explain (summarize the main idea), and Link (connect to the closing).

concepts.title: The Danger of Using Clichés in Article Writing
concepts.description: Clichés are overused expressions or phrases that can make an article seem unoriginal and boring. Students often use clichés without realizing it, so it's essential to avoid them and use fresh, creative language instead.

concepts.title: The Five W's and One H in Article Writing
concepts.description: The Five W's (Who, What, When, Where, and Why) and One H (How) are essential questions to consider when writing an article. They help to provide context and create a clear structure for the article.

concepts.title: The Importance of a Strong Closing
concepts.description: A strong closing is essential in an article as it leaves a lasting impression on the reader. It can be a thought-provoking question, a call to action, or a final thought that summarizes the main idea.

concepts.title: The Use of Examples and Evidence in Article Writing
concepts.description: Examples and evidence are crucial in article writing as they help to support the main argument or opinion. They make the article more convincing and persuasive, and help to engage the reader.
